body.title: [sprint] Ground Loop cron bootstrap — system cron trigger + cross-platform daemon orchestration
body.intention: I intend that Ground Loop provides unified daemon orchestration across all platforms, replacing per-device dispatcher logic.
body.description:
Wire system cron to trigger Ground Loop startup. On Linux/macOS: cron invokes `groundloop_start.sh` which calls `python ground_loop_daemon.py` if not already running. On Windows: Ground Loop runs as startup task or service. Ground Loop is a persistent Python process that: (1) discovers all `devices/*/groundloop/runme.py` files; (2) imports and runs them; (3) monitors for crashes; (4) auto-restarts failed daemons (or renames to .borkedpy if repeated failures); (5) detects new/removed runme.py files and hot-reloads. No Unix-specific APIs — works on Windows, macOS, Linux.

**Affected files:** devices/ground_loop/daemon.py (new), devices/ground_loop/groundloop_start.sh (new), setup cron via Ansible or manual instruction
**Design rules:** log-state-changes (daemon start/stop/crash), external-state-principle (all state in .borkedpy files, not in-memory)
**Scope boundary:** Ground Loop bootstrap only; individual runme.py implementations are separate tickets
**Completion criteria:** (1) Ground Loop process starts on system boot (cron on Linux/macOS, startup task on Windows); (2) discovers runme.py files; (3) runs them; (4) logs all state changes to datacenter_logs/ground_loop/; (5) handles Windows and Linux; (6) restarts on reboot; (7) manual start/stop via groundloop_start.sh works
**Test plan:** (1) Start Ground Loop, verify it runs; (2) Check logs for daemon discovery; (3) Stop Ground Loop, verify daemons stop; (4) Reboot (or simulate), verify Ground Loop auto-starts; (5) Test on Windows VM if available
